title: Optimize non-blocking assignments to unpacked arrays into vector applications
body:
Currently everywhere we have an NBA to an array location, we generate a separate memory-like address/value/write_enable group of signals (though the write_enable is sometimes shared). An alternative would be to push the target address and value on the back of a queue (a std::vector), and then commit the updates in order and clear the queue in the AstAlwaysPost.

This would make NBAs to arrays always work inside procedural loops that are not unrolled, reduce code size in the AstAlwaysPost and make them more alike the NBAs to non-unpacked-array variables for the purposes of scheduling with the planned changes in #3278 .

There is a trade-off between using a vector or a hash map to hold the pending updates. Assuming concurrent updates to the same address are rare, a vector is likely more efficient.
